what_it_does: Documents the 16 GiB VRAM allocation plan for an NVIDIA RTX 4080, listing verified co-resident model services, single-service swap candidates, untested co-resident frontiers, unexpected memory consumers, throughput tradeoffs, and operational rules.
observations: Effective substrate VRAM is ~15.4 GiB after ~900 MiB idle overhead.

Default verified co-resident set is vllm-chat (Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ ~12.8 GiB), vllm-embed (BGE-M3 ~2.7 GiB), and vllm-jina-v4 (~7.4 GiB BF16 weights), combining to ~15.5 GiB because vLLM memory-utilization budgets are share-of-free-VRAM, not absolute reservations.
The three-service default sustained concurrency 32 on chat and embed and 8+ minute mixed-load runs without OOM.
Single-service swap candidates near 12 GB weights leave very tight headroom for KV cache and low concurrency.
Untested co-resident frontiers (e.g., adding jina-v5-text-small) are expected to exceed budget.

Unexpected VRAM consumers include vision towers on VL models, conservative CUDAGraphs memory-profiler estimates, KV cache scaling with max-num-seqs × max-model-len, Mamba caches, and Docker/X server overhead.
Throughput/capacity tradeoff: 4K/64 seqs for throughput vs 32K/8 seqs for long context.
Operational rules include not adding a fourth service, lowering max-num-seqs before raising gpu-memory-utilization above 0.90, and checking nvidia-smi before startup.
standouts: The default three-service co-resident pattern sits at 95% VRAM utilization and has been load-tested.
vLLM's --gpu-memory-utilization values are share-of-free-VRAM budgets, which explains why three services totaling ~22.9 GiB naive sum fit in ~15.5 GiB.
CUDAGraphs memory-profiler over-reservation is explicitly disabled via environment variable.
conventions: Uses a Markdown table-heavy operations log format with cross-links to model and operations pages.
Distinguishes verified, single-service, and untested patterns with explicit risk labels.
Includes an 'Open at this topic' section for unresolved questions and measurement gaps.
concerns: Some numbers appear inconsistent or rounded (e.g., jina-v4 BF16 weights 7.4 GiB plus chat 12.8 GiB plus embed 2.7 GiB already exceed 15.5 GiB, so the sharing explanation is asserted but not quantified).
Untested co-resident frontiers are speculative and could mislead if read as recommendations.
Open questions remain unconfirmed, including WSL/Windows memory ballooning effects and lack of continuous VRAM tracker.
notes: This is the operational VRAM ledger for a 16 GiB GPU: it records what fits, what almost fits, and the rules for not running out of memory.
questions: What is the exact VRAM attribution for jina-v4 when co-resident (BF16 weights 7.4 GiB vs observed combined 15.5 GiB)?
How was the 8+ minute mixed-load test defined (request mix, throughput, error criteria)?
What does the 0.27 GiB KV at 2K context / max concurrency 2.4x metric mean for the 9B VL swap candidate?
Is the effective idle usage of ~900 MiB stable across WSL/Windows configurations?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a VRAM budget and allocation patterns; the file delivers exactly that, plus operational rules and open questions.
apparent_themes: Memory budgeting as a first-class operational constraint.
Explicit risk labeling for untested configurations.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn empirical GPU memory observations into a durable operational playbook so the team can swap models and add services without OOM surprises.
novelty: Treats vLLM memory-utilization budgets as share-of-free-VRAM rather than fixed reservations, and documents the resulting non-naive co-residency math.
contribution: A concrete, load-tested 16 GiB co-residency recipe with explicit rules, anti-patterns, and open measurement gaps for memory planning.
summary_for_embedding: Operations ledger documenting 16 GiB VRAM allocation on an RTX 4080, including verified co-resident vLLM services, swap candidates, untested frontiers, unexpected memory consumers, and operational rules.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a memory-budget reference and operational guardrails for running multiple model services on a single 16 GiB GPU.
